title: eriodictyon parryi
intro: Eriodictyon parryi, also known as California yerba santa, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an expectorant, relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. In herbal medicine, Eriodictyon parryi is often used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and colds, and is also valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
